rules.trigger: Heavy tube/LDC mic on lightweight stand
rules.warning: Heavy tube/LDC mics require heavy-duty stands. Using a light stand risks tipping and fatal damage.
rules.explanation: Large tube/LDC mics can weigh 500g-1kg+. Lightweight stands tip easily. One fall can destroy a $5k+ mic.
rules.safeAlternative: Always use a heavy-duty boom stand with wide tripod base for large condenser/tube mics.

rules.warning: Never apply phantom power to a ribbon mic - immediate damage risk!
rules.explanation: Phantom power can irreversibly destroy ribbon mic motors in seconds.
rules.safeAlternative: Check mic type before powering. Ribbon mics NEVER need phantom power.

rules.trigger: Moving mic stand at full extension
rules.warning: Top-heavy mic stands tip easily if moved fully extended.
rules.explanation: Abrupt movement shifts center of gravity and can cause a fall.
rules.safeAlternative: Lower the stand before repositioning, then re-extend.

rules.warning: Wrapping cables tightly around stands damages them over time.
rules.explanation: Tight wrapping stresses inner conductors and cracks insulation.
rules.safeAlternative: Use over-under coiling. Secure with velcro ties, not around hardware.

rules.warning: Plugging XLR at high gain sends loud pops to speakers - risking damage.
rules.explanation: Sudden transients can damage drivers, especially with high-gain preamps.
rules.safeAlternative: Bring all gain/faders down before connecting/disconnecting.

rules.warning: Cables across walkways are trip hazards and prone to damage.
rules.explanation: Foot traffic abrades cable jackets and creates safety risks.
rules.safeAlternative: Route along walls or use a cable ramp across walkways.

rules.warning: Stacking gear improperly risks overheating and mechanical damage.
rules.explanation: Blocked vents = overheating; stacked weight = broken cases; risk of falling.
rules.safeAlternative: Place in rack mounts or on flat, supported surfaces. Never stack tube gear.

rules.warning: Tube gear must never have blocked ventilation - fire and failure risk.
rules.explanation: Tube preamps/amps make lots of heat. Blocked air leads to shutdown or fire.
rules.safeAlternative: Allow 2U clearance above and behind tube gear. Never cover vents.

rules.trigger: Bags or gear on stand legs
rules.warning: Bags/gear on stand legs can tip stands unexpectedly.
rules.explanation: False sense of stability - real safety comes from leg angle and surface.
rules.safeAlternative: Only use approved counterweights; tripod legs fully spread on level ground.

rules.warning: Spills on consoles and keyboards cause instant, often fatal damage.
rules.explanation: Liquid = short circuits; repairs are rarely possible.
rules.safeAlternative: Only use covered cups, kept OFF gear surfaces at all times.

rules.trigger: Dangling cables or adapters stressing connectors
rules.warning: Dangling cables or adapters stress connectors and cause failures.
rules.explanation: Mechanical load wrecks connector pins and jacks, causing failures.
rules.safeAlternative: Always support cables, use clips/ties so no strain on connectors.

rules.warning: Raising gain before confirming signal can send noise/hum to monitors.
rules.explanation: Feedback, noise, or sudden loud signals can damage speakers.
rules.safeAlternative: Always verify signal chain with minimal gain, then raise slowly.

rules.warning: Never set master fader to full before audio check - speaker-destroying levels possible!
rules.explanation: Full master output = instant, possibly irreparable driver damage.
rules.safeAlternative: Monitors to minimum, then raise gradually only after confirming inputs.

rules.trigger: Swapping mics with phantom power on
rules.warning: Swapping mics while phantom is on sends damaging pulses - destroying sensitive mics.
rules.explanation: Phantom transients can arc through mic components.
rules.safeAlternative: Always disable phantom, wait 10s, then swap.
